response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with broad polyglot experience, but the resume shows minimal alignment with the core requirements of this role. Java appears only as a listed skill with brief mentions at Altom and National Instruments (a decade ago), there is no Kotlin experience, no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate work, and no demonstrated IDE-internals or JVM language-tooling background. The strongest IDE-adjacent experience (LabWindows/CVI in C/C++) is from 2014-2015. Recent work is centered on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, which do not transfer to the deep JVM expertise this role demands.
strengths: Long track record on complex systems and large-scale codebases across multiple domains
Brief IDE-adjacent experience contributing C/C++ components to LabWindows/CVI at National Instruments
Strong software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, custom test runner)
concerns: No demonstrated Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate or enterprise JVM framework experience
No Kotlin experience and only peripheral, dated Java exposure
No evidence of IDE internals, language tooling, or JVM-level work in recent roles

key_factors.factor: No Kotlin and shallow Java background
Prior IDE component work at National Instruments
